meta.title: Lev Route Gates: Operator-Ready Proof
meta.description: Project onboarding route gates moved from design to verified fixtures with lev-ralph, Pentagon, UltraQA, and community examples evidence.

props.title: The route gate is working
props.subtitle: Lev can now scan a project shape, choose a non-linear promotion route, render an operator card, generate local FlowMind route files, and register monitor intent without forcing plugin-first work.

props.content: Five slices moved through `lev-ralph` and landed with receipts. The end state is not daemon scheduling promotion yet; it is operator-ready HITL review with a verified community example and adversarial proof harness.

props.label: VERIFIED SIGNALS
props.title: What changed from design to proof
props.subtitle: The important number is not just green tests. It is how many false-green paths now fail closed.

props.label: SHIPPED SURFACE
props.title: The onboarding route is now a bounded operator loop
props.subtitle: Seed material no longer has to pretend every idea is a plugin candidate.

props.title: Project profile + route schema
props.content: Workshop now has typed route/profile contracts for project shape, owner, blast radius, proof, alternatives, next actions, and invalid route rejection.

props.title: Project-local FlowMind files
props.content: Fixture repos emit `.lev/flows/onboarding.flow.yaml` and `.lev/flows/route-gate.flow.yaml`; ordinary repos use `.lev/workshop` without inheriting Lev's top-level workshop overlay.

props.title: Operator route card
props.content: The route card shows source refs, observations, recommendation, rejected alternatives, confidence, owner, proof needed, and enabled next actions.

props.title: Monitor intent, not daemon claims
props.content: Schedule intent loads through `plugins/scheduling` from project config or `.schedule.yaml`; daemon lifecycle remains explicitly outside this proof.

props.title: Community Pentagon harness
props.content: `community/examples/project-onboarding-route-gates` now exercises fixture matrix, ratchets, UltraQA scenarios, and anti-slop checks.

props.label: RECEIPTS
props.title: Each slice closed with executable evidence
props.subtitle: The receipts are append-only and point at gates, reviews, artifacts, and claim verdicts.

rows.outcome: Project profile + route schema. Gate pass; Codex review pass.
Route-gate FlowMind generation. Gate pass; Codex quota blocked; fallback review recorded.
rows.evidence: FlowMind route-gate generation, bounded exits, seed matrix

rows.outcome: Workshop route card projection. Gate pass; Claude Haiku review pass.
rows.evidence: 62/62 route-card and fixture tests plus pipeline verifier

rows.outcome: Schedule intent monitor path. Gate pass; Claude Haiku review pass.
rows.evidence: 51/51 scheduling tests, monitor action, no-daemon-claim check

rows.outcome: Pentagon + UltraQA harness. Gate pass; Codex Spark review pass.

props.label: COVERAGE
props.title: The proof targets routing bias, not just happy paths
props.subtitle: A route card saying SUCCESS is not enough; the harness checks structured proofs and nonzero exits.

rows.signal: core route; shared contracts do not park in plugin; confidence 0.90
poc route; feature shaping stays lightweight; confidence 0.76
worktree route; broad churn is isolated; confidence 0.79
plugin route; extension boundary is real; confidence 0.83
interview route; low confidence stops execution; confidence 0.58
monitor route; watch signal without overclaiming; confidence 0.72
core route; local overlay does not leak to default repos; confidence 0.86

props.label: CONTROL LOOP
props.title: The model that survived the spike
props.subtitle: Workshop owns the operator decision surface; FlowMind owns executable local flows; Scheduling owns schedule declarations.

props.title: Route gate onboarding loop
props.content:
flowchart TD
  Seed[Seed material] --> Scan[Project scan]
  Scan --> Profile[Project profile]
  Profile --> Gate[Route gate]
  Gate --> Card[Operator route card]
  Card --> Flows[Local FlowMind files]
  Card --> Action[Next action]
  Action --> Proof[Receipts + gate proofs]
  Proof --> Gate
  Card --> Schedule[Monitor intent]
  Schedule -. no daemon claim .-> Proof

props.label: DOGFOOD NOTES
props.title: How lev-ralph performed
props.subtitle: The flow got the work done, and the sharp edges were useful because they became fixes or clear product notes.

props.title: Core loop held
props.content: Work, gate, and review produced usable receipts across five slices. Composer 2.5 Fast was effective as the worker lane; Claude Haiku and Codex Spark both worked as reviewers when available.

props.title: Review scope needed to be first-class
props.content: A dirty multi-workstream repo caused unrelated review noise. `lev-ralph` now supports `review_scope` so reviewers inspect scoped pathspecs instead of the whole worktree.

props.title: Branch tokens must be strict
props.content: One substantive PASS came back as Markdown and failed the branch-token contract. The prompt now requires a bare branch token with no labels, punctuation, or extra words.

props.title: Provider unavailable is not a review
props.content: Codex hit a usage-limit diagnostic on S2. The harness now detects provider-unavailable output instead of laundering it into reviewer branch text.

props.title: `lev flowmind --list` should not compile everything
props.content: The current command found 177 files, compiled 145, failed 32, and exited 1. Listing should be a non-mutating inventory with validity badges, filters, and a separate validate/compile action.

props.label: NEXT OPERATOR MOVE
props.title: Ready for review, not overclaimed
props.subtitle: The next step is to wire this into intake/propose/interview-auto as the default route card experience.

props.content:
The useful boundary is now visible: CDO, intake, capture, papers, bugs, parity rows, and chat ideas can all become seed material for the same route gate. The gate decides whether the next move is core, plugin, spike, worktree spike, monitor, interview, or reject.

That is the sane default for broad consumption: scan the project first, compile a project-local route policy, show the operator the decision, then run only the actions that have owner and proof.

props.title: Known limits remain explicit
props.content: Daemon scheduling is not part of this proof. S2 used a recorded fallback review after Codex quota blocked the requested review. S5 records the known pre-release proof-spine gap where intent conservation can fail while declared gate/review proofs pass.
